label: Berkshire's Performance vs. S&P
description: The letter begins by presenting a detailed table comparing Berkshire Hathaway's annual percentage change in per-share market value against the S&P 500's performance with dividends included, spanning from 1965 to 2021.

label: Shareholder Communication Philosophy
description: Buffett emphasizes the importance of direct and transparent communication with shareholders, treating them equally and maximizing their time to absorb information.
key_quote: Our policy is to treat all shareholders equally.

label: Intrinsic Value Focus
description: The primary duty is increasing the intrinsic value of shares, achieved through long-term business performance rather than short-term market moves.
key_quote: That task has been my primary duty for 57 years.

label: Business-Picking over Stock-Picking
description: Berkshire focuses on investing in businesses with durable economic advantages and first-class CEOs, rather than simply picking stocks for timely market moves.
key_quote: Charlie and I are not stock-pickers; we are business-pickers.

label: Infrastructure Asset Supremacy
description: Berkshire owns and operates more U.S.-based infrastructure assets than any other American corporation, a fact that has emerged organically.
key_quote: Berkshire owns and operates more U.S.-based “infrastructure” assets...than are owned and operated by any other American corporation.

label: Partnership with Government
description: Berkshire acknowledges the financial partnership between the company and the U.S. government through substantial tax payments and the benefits of operating in America.
key_quote: Berkshire’s prosperity has been fostered mightily because the company has operated in America.

label: Insurance Float Advantage
description: Berkshire's insurance float has grown significantly and has proven to be a valuable and relatively cost-free source of investment capital.
key_quote: So far, this float has cost us less than nothing.
